title: Galarhoeus paralias

The Cucurbitaceae family includes various types of vining plants, including cucumbers, melons, and gourds. Galarhoeus paralias is native to the coastal areas of Japan and Korea. This plant is often found growing in sandy dunes and other coastal habitats.

Galarhoeus tetragonocarpus is a type of succulent plant, also known as a leafy stem succulent, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Galarhoeus tetragonocarpus include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
